ogTitle: Simpkins Creek vs Singleton Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Simpkins Creek and Singleton Heights.
heading: Simpkins Creek vs Singleton Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Simpkins Creek, New South Wales and Singleton Heights, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Simpkins Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Singleton Heights (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Simpkins Creek or Singleton Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Simpkins Creek has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Singleton Heights scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Simpkins Creek scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Singleton Heights scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Simpkins Creek or Singleton Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Simpkins Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
